docs.text: Welcome to CASTLE Docs This is the official documentation for all AU CASTLE Lab services and practices. Planned Categories Lab Practices - Meeting agendas, governance, etc. Lab Equipment - An inventory of equipment, with detailed pages as needed. Lab PC Build - How to prepare and deploy the Linux image used on lab workstations. Server Setup - How to set up and maintain services including LDAP, PLA, Docs, NFS, Docker images, etc.
docs.title: Home

docs.title: Planned Categories

docs.text: About CASTLE The CASTLE Lab weekly meetings are a place for students who are interested in computing related topics to socialize and share knowledge outside of the classroom, and to join extracurricular projects and events related to computing (and computer science). The CASTLE Lab is also open for students to use during the day as a social and work space. We hope to see you at our next meeting! Acronym CASTLE stands for the Computer And Software Teaching-Learning Experience.
docs.title: About

docs.text: CASTLE PC Build This page describes how to set up and deploy the CASTLE PC Build for the lab workstations. Packages to install with apt vim build-essential gdb supertuxkart armagetronad idle screen tmux htop Packages to install by other means vscode dbeaver

docs.text: CASTLE Server Infrastructure This page describes how to set up and maintain services used in the CASTLE Lab. OS installation Install latest Debian(13) RAID install mdadm: sudo apt install mdadm Clear the old partition tables from the disks: sudo dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/nvme0n1 bs=4096 count=1000 sudo dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/nvme1n1 bs=4096 count=1000 sudo dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/nvme2n1 bs=4096 count=1000 Then create the RAID 5 array: sudo mdadm --create --verbose /dev/md0 --level=5 --raid-devices=3 /dev/nvme0n1 /dev/nvme1n1 /dev/nvme2n1
docs.title: Server

docs.text: Install latest Debian(13)

docs.text: install mdadm: sudo apt install mdadm Clear the old partition tables from the disks: sudo dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/nvme0n1 bs=4096 count=1000 sudo dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/nvme1n1 bs=4096 count=1000 sudo dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/nvme2n1 bs=4096 count=1000 Then create the RAID 5 array: sudo mdadm --create --verbose /dev/md0 --level=5 --raid-devices=3 /dev/nvme0n1 /dev/nvme1n1 /dev/nvme2n1
